info.name: UNIT TEST
info.description:
Complete test suite for https://api.restful-api.dev

Endpoints covered:
  GET    /objects
  GET    /objects/{id}
  POST   /objects
  PUT    /objects/{id}
  PATCH  /objects/{id}
  DELETE /objects/{id}

Coverage per endpoint:
  • Happy Path
  • Negative
  • Edge Case
  • Business Logic (response time < 1000ms + schema validation)

Integration Flows:
  • Full CRUD (6 steps)
  • List and Retrieve (3 steps)

No authentication required.

exec: pm.test('Response is a non-empty array', function () {
pm.test('Each object has id (string) and name (string)', function () {
// Store ALL object IDs as a JSON array in the 'id' collection variable

item.name: [GET] /objects - Happy Path: Filter object by random an ID

exec: console.warn('Pre-request: collection variable "id" is empty — run GET /objects first.');
pm.test('All requested IDs are present in the response', function () {
pm.test('No IDs returned outside the requested filter', function () {

item.name: [GET] /objects - Happy Path: Filter objects by multiple random IDs

exec: console.warn('Pre-request: collection variable "id" is empty — run GET /objects first.');
pm.test('All requested IDs are present in the response', function () {
pm.test('No IDs returned outside the requested filter', function () {
pm.test('Returns empty array for non-existent filter ID', function () {

item.name: [GET] /objects - Edge Case: Filter by mixed existent and non-existent IDs

exec: console.warn('Pre-request: "id" is empty — run GET /objects first.');
pm.test('Existent ID is returned in response', function () {
pm.test('Non-existent ID is not present in response', function () {
pm.test('Response contains only the one existent object', function () {

item.name: [GET] /objects - Edge Case: Filter by duplicate IDs

exec: console.warn('Pre-request: "id" is empty — run GET /objects first.');
pm.test('Duplicate IDs do not cause a server error', function () {
pm.test('Duplicate IDs do not produce duplicate rows', function () {

item.name: [GET] /objects - Business Logic: Full array schema and response time

exec: pm.test('Schema: every object has id (string), name (string), data (object|null)', function () {
pm.test('No unexpected top-level keys on list items', function () {

exec: console.warn('Pre-request: "id" is empty — run GET /objects first.');
pm.test('Returned id matches the requested random ID', function () {

exec: pm.test('data field is explicitly null', function () {
pm.test('id and name are still present', function () {
pm.test('Error body has error field', function () {
pm.test('Status code is 4xx for invalid ID format', function () {
pm.test('ID in response matches requested ID', function () {

pm.test('Schema: only allowed top-level keys present', function () {

pm.test('Name is echoed back correctly', function () {
pm.test('data fields match submitted payload', function () {

item.name: [POST] /objects - Happy Path: Create object with null data

exec: pm.test('data field is null in response', function () {
pm.test('Returns 4xx or creates without name (API permissive check)', function () {
// API may return 400/422 or create the object with no name
pm.test('API handles empty body without 5xx crash', function () {
pm.test('Returns 400 or 500 for malformed JSON', function () {
pm.test('Server returns a parseable response body', function () {

item.name: [POST] /objects - Edge Case: Name with special and Unicode characters

exec: pm.test('Special characters preserved in name', function () {

item.name: [POST] /objects - Edge Case: Very long name (300 characters)

exec: pm.test('API handles 300-char name without 5xx crash', function () {
pm.test('Returns 200 or validation error for oversized name', function () {

item.name: [POST] /objects - Edge Case: Deeply nested data payload

exec: pm.test('Deeply nested data is stored and returned intact', function () {

item.name: [POST] /objects - Business Logic: Auto ID, ISO createdAt, no updatedAt

exec: pm.test('createdAt is a valid ISO 8601 timestamp', function () {
pm.test('createdAt is within the last 60 seconds', function () {
pm.test('updatedAt is NOT present on a fresh create', function () {

item.name: [PUT] /objects/{id} - Happy Path: Full replacement of existing object

item.name: [PATCH] /objects/{id} - Happy Path: Partial update — name only

pm.test('Name is updated to the patched value', function () {
pm.test('data is preserved after name-only patch', function () {

pm.test('data reflects the patched values', function () {
pm.test('name is unchanged after data-only patch', function () {
pm.test('Error body has error field', function () {
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ name: 'PATCH Edge Object', data: { x: 1 } }) }
pm.test('Empty patch does not cause 5xx crash', function () {
pm.test('Returns 200 or 400 for empty patch body', function () {
